reviews.review_description: I dunno what she was complaining about.
You can't write a bad review because you will be considered anti-semite. My previous review was deleted.

I thoroughly enjoyed this.  It made me re-learn 4th grade history...or 5th or 6th

I'm 35 yrs old now and it actually held my attention. Thank you VR!
Such a great medium to get a sense of the space she lived in. Educational. Emotional. Free.

A breath of fresh air when in comparison to most the other APPs on the Go.   It actually looks decent.   It is unbelievable that a subpar graphics interface is getting a looks decent but when the norm for the Oculus Go is blurry then its the best that the headset has to offer.   It actually works.  With so many thing that I download not even working its amazing that I don’t send this crap back and get a refund.   It is however a good change of pace from broken.

My wife always wanted to visit this place, but it is quite far away from us (we live in Colombia), so this was the best way to show her this place while we find a way to visit it in person. From a technical point of view, the app worked perfectly because it didn't overheat the device like other VR apps and the quality and the sound are impressive.
Japanese kids want Japanese translation！
Very moving had a tear awful people Germans back in the day well put together experience be ready to cry people very sad and interesting respect to devs Job well done.

This is truly an amazing experience. The tour is quite moving, and having visited the real house myself, I must say the VR tour is incredibly well done. The story lives forever, hoping to change people’s hearts and educate against senseles prejudice and racism. A must for everyone.

I experienced this app on Oculus go. It is exceptionally well done. Very emotional. You will definitely Cry by the end of the story. I will strongly recommend that everybody experience this story. What a lovely and brave human Ann Frank was.
The app is very very done. You actually feel like you are in that Annex.
Great job Developers. This was a free app but I would have gladly paid money for it.
The story mode in particular is very well done.
The experience to see the Anne Frank story is VR was amazing. I really recommend to download this one and experience it for yourself.

There's no way you will ever get closer to Anne Frank and her story than this app, unless you visit the real house. This app has amazing graphics and really makes you feel like you are inside the house. Brought to life in a way that only VR can do. Absolutely a fantastic app for those who enjoy lessons in history!

This is truly an amazing experience. The tour is very moving. I've never visited the house myself in real life, but I'd sure love to in the future. the ending does infuriate me though. The tour really puts you in their shoes and makes you feel very uneasy and appreciate of society today.
Great job.. is fantastic... i feel the atmosphere.. whith the sound the images... thanks by the spanish language... more than this..
This was really interesting but sad to hear what it was lol for her before her demise. I’d recommend it for any history fans.

A well done narration of the story of Anne Frank.the graphics were amazing and detailed. I felt like i was inside the actual rooms and got to know the story on a much more deeper level. The story was one that was full of hope for a young girl. Really a wonderful adaptation, well worth the download. You won't be disappointed.

reviews.review_title: This touched my heart into the core!
reviews.review_description:
I never read any stories but i love listening to it and with VR it felt like i was there and she was telling me her stories. Everything is so detailed and the way they represented it with voice and 3D objects is just ah so much more than i expected.

I felt so sad in the end and it brought tears into my eyes. We don't want anybody go through this ever again like anne did, nobody should. Despite the situation it's lovely to dream but it hardly comes true in the end.

I visited Amsterdam years ago but never had the pleasure to visit the Anne Frank house.  The way the VR tour is presented is so well done, that I could almost imagine being there during her time.  What a wonderful way to bring one of our most important pieces of history into the present, so we will never forget!
How can they offer free high quality apps like this?
